name: Alex Dathskovsky
bio: 17+ years of software development experience across embedded systems, big data, and high-performance computing. Currently Group/Technical Manager at Speedata.io. Previously held roles at Abbott Israel, Axxana (Technical Leader), Rafael (Senior SW Developer), and Elbit Systems.

name: Ankur M Satle
bio: Architect at EXFO working on telecom products (3G/4G/5G/IMS/VoIP, Network Monitoring, OSS/BSS) with 20 years of experience. Passionate about code that is easy-to-use-correctly, correct-by-construction, and minimalistic. Has spoken at CppCon 2020, 2021, and 2022. Active CppIndia organiser and presenter.

name: John Stratton
topic: Software Design and Performance in C++
bio: CTO of MulticoreWare and Associate Professor of Computer Science at Whitman College. Holds a Ph.D. in Electrical & Computer Engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (2013). Designed MxPA — the first licensable implementation of OpenCL. Published research on Halide and OpenCL compilers for DSP and scientific computing.

name: Jonathan Müller
topic: An (In-)Complete Guide to C++ Object Lifetimes
bio: Software Engineer at think-cell maintaining core libraries. Creator of several popular open-source C++ projects: type_safe, foonathan/memory, lexy (parser combinator), and lauf (bytecode interpreter). Member of the C++ standardization committee — assistant chair for std::ranges.

name: Mahendra Bhagwan Garodi
bio: 10+ years developing virtual platforms in the semiconductor industry, focusing on CPU/memory/peripheral modeling in SoCs — both functional and microarchitectural. Active CppIndia contributor presenting on SIMD, performance evaluation, and modern C++. Also teaches C, C++, Python, Data Structures, and Mathematics.

name: Mike Shah
topic: Getting Started with Modern C++ — A Tour of Features
bio: Teaching Professor focusing on computer systems, graphics, and software engineering. Research areas include performance engineering (dynamic analysis), software visualization, and computer graphics. Also works as a 3D Senior Graphics Engineer in C++. Active educator with a popular YouTube channel.

name: Pavithra Ashok Kumar
bio: Software engineer with ~10 years of experience across C++, MATLAB, OCaml, and Java. Loves abstractions and talking to the compiler. Passionate about systematic debugging approaches and writing correct-by-construction software.

name: Šimon Tóth
bio: Ex-software engineer and researcher with 20 years of industry experience. Author of the 'Daily bit(e) of C++' series, 'A Complete Guide to Standard C++ Algorithms' book, and 'Surviving the C++ Coding Interview'. Focused on making modern C++ features accessible to all developers.

name: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach
bio: Principal Engineer at NVIDIA and Chair of the ISO C++ Library Evolution Working Group. One of the most active contributors to the C++ standardization process. Co-chair of the C++ Standard Library working group and a frequent keynote speaker at CppCon and other major C++ conferences.

name: Mike Shah
bio: Teaching Professor focusing on computer systems, graphics, and software engineering. Research areas include performance engineering (dynamic analysis), software visualization, and computer graphics. Popular C++ educator with a YouTube channel reaching thousands of developers.

name: Kiran Kannappan
bio: Software engineer at Intel specializing in heterogeneous computing and the oneAPI unified programming model. Works on enabling developers to write high-performance C++ code targeting CPUs, GPUs, and FPGAs using SYCL and the Intel oneAPI toolkit.

name: Jonathan Müller
topic: C++ Features You Might Not Know
bio: Software Engineer at think-cell. Creator of popular open-source C++ libraries including type_safe, foonathan/memory, and lexy. Active C++ standardization committee member and technical writer covering modern C++ features.

name: Venkata Naga Ravikiran Bulusu
bio: Software engineer with deep expertise in concurrent and parallel programming in C++. Focused on practical concurrency patterns — thread pools, producer-consumer models, readers-writer locks — and making multi-threaded C++ accessible and correct.

name: Ankur M Satle
bio: Architect at EXFO with 20 years of experience in telecom software. Passionate advocate for value semantics and declarative C++. Regular speaker at CppCon and CppIndia, and an active organiser of CppIndia events.

name: Andreas Fertig
bio: Independent C++ trainer, consultant, and author based in Germany. Author of 'Programming with C++20' and creator of C++ Insights — a tool that shows how the compiler transforms your C++ source. Specializes in making complex modern C++ features understandable through concrete examples.

name: Kushashwa Ravi Shrimali
topic: Using C++ for Your Artificial Intelligence Solution
bio: ML/AI software engineer passionate about high-performance computing and the intersection of C++ and machine learning. Contributes to open-source ML frameworks and writes about practical C++ for AI/ML workloads. Author of various C++ and OpenCV tutorials.

name: Selvakumar Jawahar
topic: Why 5G is Fast? and C++ in 5G Open Source
bio: Software engineer working in the telecommunications domain with hands-on experience in 5G open-source stacks. Uses C++ extensively for building low-latency, high-throughput radio access network components. Active contributor to open-source 5G projects.

name: Prince Gupta
bio: Software engineer interested in compiler optimizations and low-level performance tuning in C++. Presented a lightning talk at CppIndiaCon 2023 on writing code that lets the compiler automatically vectorize loops using SIMD instructions.

name: Emmanuel Danso Nyarko
topic: Understanding Dynamic Allocators for Optimization in Containers
bio: Software engineer focused on memory management and performance optimization in C++. Delivered a lightning talk at CppIndiaCon 2023 on custom allocators and how they can dramatically improve container performance in real-world applications.

name: Mahendra Garodi
bio: Software engineer in the semiconductor industry with 10+ years of experience. Active CppIndia community member and contributor who has delivered talks and lightning talks at multiple CppIndiaCon editions.

name: Inbal Levi
topic: Customization Methods: Connecting User And Library Code
bio: Software engineer and active ISO C++ standardization committee member. Chair of the C++ Israel local chapter and an organiser of CoreCpp Conference. Focuses on library design, customization points, and making the C++ standard library easier to extend correctly.

name: Hemil Ruparel
topic: Default Generated Comparison for Your Data Types
bio: Software engineer and CppIndia community moderator. Deep interest in C++20 features and library design. Presented at CppIndiaCon 2022 on the C++20 spaceship operator and compiler-generated comparison operators.

name: Selvakumar Jawahar
bio: Software engineer in the telecommunications domain with deep expertise in event-driven concurrency. Presented at both CppIndiaCon 2022 and 2023, covering the Reactor pattern and 5G open-source stacks.

name: Dr. Greg Law
topic: Getting the Most Out of GDB
bio: Co-founder and CTO of Undo — creators of the UDB time-travel debugger for Linux C/C++ applications. An authority on debugging technology with decades of experience building tools that let developers record and replay program execution. Frequent speaker at CppCon and other conferences.

name: Ankur M Satle
bio: Architect at EXFO with 20 years of experience in telecom software. Active CppIndia organiser who has presented at three consecutive CppIndiaCon editions (2021, 2022, 2023). Advocates for modern C++ idioms that make manual memory management unnecessary.

name: Bjarne Stroustrup
bio: Creator of C++ and author of 'The C++ Programming Language'. Currently a Technical Fellow at Morgan Stanley and a Visiting Professor at Columbia University. Has shaped the C++ language for over 40 years, leading its design and standardization through C++11, C++14, C++17, C++20, and beyond.

name: Jon Kalb
topic: C++ Today: The Beast is Back
bio: Chair of CppCon — the world's largest C++ conference. Long-time C++ trainer and author. Has given the opening keynote at CppCon multiple times and is known for his ability to convey the energy and momentum of the modern C++ ecosystem to developers worldwide.

name: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach
topic: What Belongs in the C++ Standard Library
bio: Principal Engineer at NVIDIA and Chair of the ISO C++ Library Evolution Working Group (LEWG). One of the most prolific contributors to the C++ standard, responsible for features in C++14, C++17, C++20, and beyond. Also co-chairs the C++ committee's concurrency study group.

name: Harishankar Singh
topic: C++20 Ranges: Code Expressively with Ranges
bio: C++ developer and community member passionate about modern C++ features. Presented at the inaugural CppIndiaCon 2021 on C++20 Ranges — covering views, adaptors, and how the ranges library makes algorithm composition more readable and composable.

name: Ankur M Satle
topic: C++20 Concepts — The Base Classes of Generic Programming?
bio: Architect at EXFO and a founding member of CppIndia. Presented at all four editions of CppIndiaCon (2021–2024). Has also spoken at CppCon on related topics in modern C++ and generic programming.

name: Saurabh Joshi
topic: Contract Programming: What, Why, and How?
bio: C++ developer and educator with a background in formal methods and software correctness. Presented at CppIndiaCon 2021 on Design by Contract in C++ — covering preconditions, postconditions, and invariants as a means of writing more correct and self-documenting code.
